diff --git a/ESP32_wifi/src/main.cpp b/ESP32_wifi/src/main.cpp
index eb3fe63..66b1df2 100644
--- a/ESP32_wifi/src/main.cpp
+++ b/ESP32_wifi/src/main.cpp
@@ -413,17 +413,20 @@ void loop() {
                 uint8_t uBuf[1024];
                 int n = uart.read(uBuf, sizeof(uBuf));
                 dbg_uartBytesRx += n;
+                // Process ALL bytes 鈥?never break early, to avoid leaving
+                // the frame parser in a partial state that causes subsequent
+                // frame loss.
                 for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                     BridgeFrame frame;
                     if (uartParser.feed(uBuf[i], frame)) {
                         dbg_uartFramesRx++;
                         if (!frame.valid) continue;
-                        if (frame.ch == BRIDGE_CH_DAP) {
+                        if (frame.ch == BRIDGE_CH_DAP && !gotResponse) {
                             dbg_dapUartRx++;
                             dapServer.sendResponse(frame.data, frame.len);
                             dbg_dapTcpSend++;
                             gotResponse = true;
-                            break;
+                            // Do NOT break 鈥?keep parsing remaining bytes
                         } else if (frame.ch == BRIDGE_CH_WIFI_CTRL) {
                             handleWifiCtrlFromMCU(frame);
                         } else {
diff --git a/app/Src/wifi_bridge.c b/app/Src/wifi_bridge.c
index 2500a1f..fe1f36e 100644
--- a/app/Src/wifi_bridge.c
+++ b/app/Src/wifi_bridge.c
@@ -119,6 +119,23 @@ void WiFi_Bridge_Send(uint8_t ch, const uint8_t *data, uint16_t len)
 
     if (osMutexAcquire(s_wifi_tx_mutex, 60U) != osOK) { return; }
 
+    /* 鈹€鈹€ Wait for previous DMA transfer to finish BEFORE touching buffer 鈹€鈹€
+     * Without this, a new caller can overwrite s_wifi_tx_buf while the
+     * DMA controller is still reading it, corrupting the on-wire frame
+     * and causing CRC failures on the ESP32 side.                        */
+    uint32_t t0 = HAL_GetTick();
+    while (HAL_UART_GetState(&huart2) & HAL_UART_STATE_BUSY_TX)
+    {
+        if ((HAL_GetTick() - t0) > 50U)
+        {
+            /* DMA stuck 鈥?abort it so we can proceed safely */
+            HAL_UART_AbortTransmit(&huart2);
+            break;
+        }
+        osDelay(1);
+    }
+
+    /* 鈹€鈹€ Now safe to fill the shared TX buffer 鈹€鈹€ */
     uint8_t crc = 0U;
     s_wifi_tx_buf[0] = BRIDGE_SOF0_RPY;            /* 0xBB */
     s_wifi_tx_buf[1] = BRIDGE_SOF1;                 /* 0x55 */
@@ -129,14 +146,6 @@ void WiFi_Bridge_Send(uint8_t ch, const uint8_t *data, uint16_t len)
     for (uint16_t i = 0U; i < len; i++) { crc ^= data[i]; }
     s_wifi_tx_buf[5U + len] = crc;
 
-    /* Wait for previous DMA transfer to finish (max 50 ms) */
-    uint32_t t0 = HAL_GetTick();
-    while (HAL_UART_GetState(&huart2) & HAL_UART_STATE_BUSY_TX)
-    {
-        if ((HAL_GetTick() - t0) > 50U) { break; }
-        osDelay(1);
-    }
-
     HAL_StatusTypeDef txrc = HAL_UART_Transmit_DMA(&huart2, s_wifi_tx_buf, (uint16_t)(6U + len));
     if (txrc == HAL_OK) { s_dbg_uart2_tx_ok++; }
     else                { s_dbg_uart2_tx_fail++; }
